public_boundary: NordSym runs on architecture it built. Some of it is public. Most of it is not. This file lists the systems NordSym deliberately makes visible.

visible_systems.name: APIClaw
visible_systems.one_liner: API and provider gateway for agents: discovery, routing, and managed calls without putting every provider key inside the agent.

visible_systems.name: Agent Badge
visible_systems.one_liner: Identity and receipts for agent work: who acted, under what mandate, and what record proves it.
visible_systems.public_note: No standalone public frontend is claimed. Public verification surfaces should be linked only when deliberately exposed.

visible_systems.name: skillsync
visible_systems.one_liner: Open-source skill distribution for keeping agent instructions synchronized across runtimes.

visible_systems.name: AI Search
visible_systems.one_liner: Checks how AI systems find, read, and cite a company.

visible_systems.name: GenPRD
visible_systems.one_liner: Turns a product idea into a build-ready product requirements document.

visible_systems.name: FlowVault
visible_systems.one_liner: Workflow library and reliability layer for n8n operations.

visible_systems.name: CleanBuddy
visible_systems.one_liner: Operations surface for cleaning companies, built and operated by NordSym for a venture company.

internal_boundary.reason: These surfaces exist to run the work, not to market it.

rules: Do not present the systems list as a product portfolio.
Do not list private client agreement surfaces as public systems.
Do not claim Agent Badge has a standalone public product frontend until a public verification surface is deliberately exposed.
Do not expose internal control-plane URLs on public discovery surfaces.
Do not include retired systems in public NordSym discovery surfaces unless they are explicitly reactivated.
